Details: In one study, the average rejection rate of simazine in a bench-scale, hollow-fiber NF membrane was 42.2%. It was shown that phenylic pesticides (Alachlor, metolachlor, methoxychlor, and thiobencarb) were rejected more effectively than non-phenylic pesticides (aldicarb, simazine, atrazine, and pirimicarb). [1430]  In another study, removal percentages for simazine by NF membranes ranged from 65 to 95% in bench-scale, flat sheet configuration.
